summary: Provision brand-new Glue catalog databases, ETL jobs, and crawlers. No curated Glue estate exists yet, so every operation is a first stand-up an engineer reviews before it applies.

operations.title: Provision a new Glue catalog database
operations.summary: Request a brand-new Glue Data Catalog database; an engineer finalizes and reviews the Terraform before it applies.
operations.description: Stand up a catalog database as a complete specification: name, description, and the default S3 location new tables use. Submitting routes it to an engineer with a draft Terraform block to review and finish — nothing is applied without review.

params.name: database_name
params.label: Database name
params.help: Lower-case letters, numbers and underscores. Names the database in the Glue Data Catalog.

params.name: description
params.label: Description
params.help: What this database holds.

params.name: location_uri
params.label: Default table location (S3)
params.help: Default S3 location new tables in this database use when none is given. Leave blank to require a location per table.

params.name: resource_name
params.label: Database name tag
params.help: Shown as the name everywhere — consoles, tickets, this portal.

params.name: description_tag
params.label: What this database holds
params.help: One line an operator can act on at 3 AM.

params.name: pic
params.label: Person in charge
params.help: Who to call about this database.

decisions: Cross-account or federated database sharing (target_database / federated_database) is an engineer decision
Default table-creation permissions (create_table_default_permission) are an engineer decision

operations.title: Provision a new Glue ETL job
operations.summary: Request a brand-new Glue Spark ETL job; an engineer finalizes and reviews the Terraform before it applies.
operations.description: Stand up an ETL job as a complete specification: script location, worker size and count, and timeout. Submitting routes it to an engineer with a draft Terraform block to review and finish — nothing is applied without review.

params.name: job_name
params.label: Job name
params.help: Names the job in the Glue console and the generated draft.

params.name: description
params.label: Description
params.help: What this job does.

params.name: role_arn
params.label: IAM role
params.help: The role Glue assumes to run this job — needs read/write access to its sources, targets, and script location.

params.name: script_location
params.label: Script location (S3)
params.help: S3 path to the ETL script this job runs.

params.name: command_name
params.label: Job type
params.help: Always the standard Spark ETL job type — written into the draft, never asked.

params.name: glue_version
params.label: Glue version
params.help: The Glue/Spark runtime version this job runs on.

params.name: worker_type
params.label: Worker type
params.help: Size class for processor and memory on every worker.

params.name: number_of_workers
params.label: Number of workers
params.help: How many workers this job run requests.

params.name: timeout_minutes
params.label: Timeout (minutes)
params.help: Minutes before a run is forcibly stopped.

params.name: max_retries
params.label: Max retries
params.help: How many times a failed run is automatically retried.

params.name: resource_name
params.label: Job name tag
params.help: Shown as the name everywhere — consoles, tickets, this portal.

params.name: description_tag
params.label: What this job does
params.help: One line an operator can act on at 3 AM.

params.name: pic
params.label: Person in charge
params.help: Who to call about this job.

decisions: Python-shell and streaming job types (command.name) are engineer decisions — this form provisions the standard Spark ETL type
Job arguments (default_arguments), connections, and a security configuration are engineer decisions
Triggers and workflow membership are engineer follow-ups after creation

operations.title: Provision a new Glue crawler
operations.summary: Request a brand-new Glue crawler over an S3 location; an engineer finalizes and reviews the Terraform before it applies.
operations.description: Stand up a crawler as a complete specification: the catalog database it populates, the IAM role it runs as, and the S3 location it scans. Submitting routes it to an engineer with a draft Terraform block to review and finish — nothing is applied without review.

params.name: crawler_name
params.label: Crawler name
params.help: Names the crawler in the Glue console and the generated draft.

params.name: database
params.label: Catalog database
params.help: The catalog database this crawler writes discovered tables into. Create the database first if the list is empty.

params.name: role
params.label: IAM role
params.help: The role Glue assumes to crawl the target and write to the catalog.

params.name: s3_target_path
params.label: S3 target path
params.help: The S3 location this crawler scans. Additional targets are an engineer follow-up.

params.name: schedule
params.label: Schedule (cron)
params.help: Leave blank to run on demand only. AWS cron syntax, e.g. cron(0 0 * * ? *).

params.name: table_prefix
params.label: Table name prefix
params.help: Prepended to every table this crawler creates. Leave blank for no prefix.

params.name: schema_update_behavior
params.label: On schema change
params.help: Whether a detected schema change updates the catalog table or only logs it.

params.name: schema_delete_behavior
params.label: On deleted object
params.help: What happens to a catalog table whose underlying data has disappeared.

params.name: resource_name
params.label: Crawler name tag
params.help: Shown as the name everywhere — consoles, tickets, this portal.

params.name: description_tag
params.label: What this crawler scans
params.help: One line an operator can act on at 3 AM.

params.name: pic
params.label: Person in charge
params.help: Who to call about this crawler.

decisions: Additional crawl targets (more S3 prefixes, JDBC, DynamoDB, Delta, Iceberg, Hudi, MongoDB) are an engineer follow-up — this form provisions the single most common S3 target
Classifiers and a security configuration are engineer decisions
Lake Formation credential mode and lineage settings are engineer decisions
